title: US PPI Forecast Snapshot — Composite Calibrated Bridge Nowcast v2.5 Enhanced
summary: Energy drag from post-Hormuz spot mean-reversion (crude -25% MoM, gasoline -3.7%) pulls headline near flat, but ISM Prices Paid at 73.0 and lagged intermediate demand keep core sticky. Normal regime (VIX ~16.4) supports tighter confidence bands. ECI Q1 interpolated at +0.30% MoM feeds services pipeline. Daily refresh — composite updated with July 6 spot inputs.
baseCase: Flat headline on energy unwind, sticky core on still-elevated ISM passthrough

release.name: PPI
release.thesis: Energy collapse from Hormuz reopening pulls headline toward zero, but still-elevated ISM Prices Paid at 73.0 and sticky services pipeline keep core above +0.2% MoM. Normal regime (VIX ~16.4) supports modest confidence bands. ECI Q1 interpolated monthly proxy (+0.30% MoM) anchors services.

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.0%, core +0.2%. Energy drag stabilizes while core goods and services stay sticky.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Rates digest the headline but focus on core. No major Fed repricing.

scenarios.label: Hotter pipeline pressure
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.3%, core +0.4%. ISM passthrough hits harder than expected; intermediate demand keeps feeding through.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Rates higher, USD firmer, margin-sensitive equities under pressure.
scenarios.invalidation: Hot print is driven by narrow commodity volatility with weak pass-through.

scenarios.label: Cleaner disinflation pulse
scenarios.outcome: Headline -0.2%, core +0.1%. Energy collapses further; core passthrough fades faster than expected.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Fed-cut odds rise, rates soften, broader equity appetite improves.
scenarios.invalidation: Market already priced a benign outcome before release.

setups.bias: Long duration if core PPI cools without hawkish spillover
setups.trigger: Core PPI <= +0.2% m/m and revisions are benign
setups.invalidation: Broad upstream re-acceleration with hawkish rates repricing

setups.bias: Constructive only if margin pressure eases
setups.trigger: Goods and services pipeline both avoid upside surprise
setups.invalidation: Input cost shock restarts (Hormuz re-closes, energy spikes)

setups.bias: Long on hotter PPI surprise with rates follow-through
setups.trigger: Headline and core both beat with higher terminal-rate pricing

watchItems.label: Final demand services
watchItems.note: A hot services print can overwhelm softer goods. Portfolio management and healthcare are slow-moving but sticky.

watchItems.label: Commodity complex
watchItems.note: Important for headline direction but not enough alone. WTI $68-74 range is the new equilibrium until Hormuz stability is fully confirmed.

watchItems.label: Pass-through
watchItems.note: Focus on whether PPI changes the CPI path or earnings narrative. Stage 1-4 intermediate demand is the leading indicator.

keyDrivers: Goods deflation helps, but services pipeline matters more for readthrough.
Commodity moves can distort the headline without changing core pass-through.
PPI matters most when it shifts margin expectations or the CPI narrative.
Hormuz reopening is a temporary supply shock reversal; geopolitical risk premium can return quickly.
risks: Consensus may already expect a benign upstream print after Hormuz deal.
Revisions can dominate the first market read. May 2026 was heavily revised.
Macro tape can be driven by yields or oil even if PPI itself is tame.
Iran deal is 60-day; collapse risk re-energizes energy PPI.
